figure.title: Security Implementation in UALink Protocol Stack
figure.purpose: Shows where encryption and integrity protections are applied within the layered UALink protocol stack.

technical_summary: The stack consists of the Accelerator Functional/Protocol layer, Transaction layer, Data link layer, and Physical layer.
Encryption/Decryption happens at the top (Accelerator Functional/Protocol layer) using AES-GCM with 256-bit keys.
It encrypts the payload and portions of the header for each transaction.
Requests, read responses, and write responses are treated as independently encrypted streams.
At the Transaction layer, if integrity is enabled, a 'tag' half flit is created and appended to each control half flit to ensure end-to-end data integrity.

interfaces.name: UPLI interface
interfaces.description: Connects Protocol layer and Transaction layer.

critical_facts: Security is implemented high in the stack, before the UPLI interface, ensuring that data is protected end-to-end as it traverses the lower fabric layers.
